r2g_i_readiness_review.resolved_blocker: R2G-J proved a localhost-only HTTP /mcp dry-run harness.

proved: approved get_project_status tool call over local HTTP

official_docs_rechecked.title: Build your MCP server
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: The MCP server owns tool definitions, auth enforcement, structured data, and tool-to-UI metadata; ChatGPT decides tool use from metadata.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Quickstart
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Local MCP inspection can use localhost, but ChatGPT development testing needs the server exposed with a public /mcp URL.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Deploy your app
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Live connector testing needs a reachable HTTPS /mcp endpoint, or an explicitly authorized local tunnel for development.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Connect from ChatGPT
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Developer-mode connector creation requires the target ChatGPT account/workspace, connector metadata, and the public /mcp URL.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Authentication
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Read-only anonymous mode may be acceptable for non-private smoke tests; user-specific data or write actions require a deliberate OAuth 2.1/auth stance.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Submit and maintain your app
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Submission and publication are separate from live smoke and require a public non-local endpoint plus review prerequisites.

official_docs_rechecked.title: Troubleshooting
official_docs_rechecked.final_prep_implication: Live smoke should check /mcp discovery, tool listing, schema alignment, auth loops, and connector metadata refresh.

allowed_after_exact_authorization_only: start one explicitly named public tunnel or use one explicitly named hosted HTTPS /mcp endpoint
create one developer-mode ChatGPT connector in the specified account/workspace
refresh connector metadata once if needed
run only allowed read-only smoke prompts/tools
shut down temporary tunnel if tunnel mode is used
still_forbidden_without_separate_authori: trigger video generation, regeneration, batch generation, final assembly, publish, submit for review, or deploy
record raw provider payloads, signed URLs, secrets, or private account data

required_fields: allowed_smoke_tests=<tools/list plus named read-only tool calls/prompts>
exact_authorization_phrase_components.draft_authorization_phrase_zh: 授权执行 1 次 ChatGPT connector live smoke：endpoint_mode=<secure_mcp_tunnel|ngrok|cloudflare_tunnel|hosted_https>，exact_mcp_url=<HTTPS /mcp URL>，chatgpt_account_or_workspace=<target>，connector_name=<name>，connector_description=<description>，auth_mode=anonymous_read_only_with_no_private_data，permission_posture=read_only_only，allowed_smoke_tests=tools/list + get_project_status + forbidden/provider tool must fail-closed，max_public_tunnels=1，max_connector_creations=1，max_metadata_refreshes=1，log_redaction_required=true，provider_calls_allowed=false，env_or_credentials_read_allowed=false，deploy_publish_submit_allowed=false，raw_payload_recording_allowed=false，stop_on_first_connector_or_auth_failure=true，完成后输出 sanitized local receipt 并关闭临时 tunnel（如果使用 tunnel）。

minimum_live_sequence_after_authorizatio: Confirm endpoint mode and exact /mcp URL from Jenn's authorization phrase.
Start only the authorized tunnel or use only the authorized hosted HTTPS endpoint.
Verify local server health and /mcp reachability without reading secrets.
Create exactly one ChatGPT connector in the authorized account/workspace.
Confirm ChatGPT lists the expected approved tools and no provider/generation tools.
Run only the authorized read-only smoke prompt/tool calls.
Record sanitized evidence without raw payloads or secrets.
Shut down the temporary tunnel if one was started.
stop_conditions: Any request would read .env, credentials, tokens, OAuth client secrets, cookies, or private account state.

Any tool path attempts RunningHub, Runway, provider upload/submit/query/download, video generation, regeneration, batch expansion, final assembly, publish, deploy, tag, release, or production configuration change.
The connector creation flow asks for a different account/workspace, connector name, endpoint, auth mode, or permission posture than authorized.
The endpoint advertises unexpected tools, provider tools, write tools without human confirmation, or schema mismatch.
The first connector creation, metadata refresh, or smoke test fails in a way that would require retry beyond the authorized counts.
